title: Shanty Open Shelf Console
summary: A plywood console split by a center divider into an open basket bay and a two-door cabinet bay — a face frame built separately and applied afterward is what gives it a finished look.
description:
The center divider does double duty: it's what turns one wide plywood box into a console with two distinct personalities — an open shelf bay sized for baskets on one side, and a closed cabinet bay behind a pair of doors on the other. Building the face frame as its own flat assembly first, then gluing and nailing it onto the finished box, is easier than trying to build the frame in place and is the same technique cabinetmakers use for exactly this reason: a flat assembly on the bench is simple to keep square, and a box you're reaching into is not.

The doors are the one place this build rewards patience. Cut them to the plan dimensions as a starting point, but fit them to your actual door openings before committing — plywood box assemblies drift by a fraction of an inch from the numbers on paper, and a door cut to the letter of the plan can end up binding on an opening that came out slightly different in practice.

tools.note: Clean crosscuts on the face frame and trim pieces.

materials.name: Plywood, 3/4", 4x8 sheet
materials.note: Or 3 - 1x12 boards @ 8 ft as a substitute for the case parts.

cutList.note: Positioned so its top is 7-3/4" from the top of the cabinet.
Cut to fit the actual opening.
Cut to fit the actual opening.

steps.title: Cut the case and trim parts
steps.body: Rip the 3/4" sheet into 11-1/4" strips — four strips per sheet — and crosscut the case parts from three of them: two sides 39-1/4", one top 39-1/2", one bottom shelf 36", one centre divider 35-3/4", and two basket-bay shelves 17-5/8". They pack three to a strip and one sheet covers all seven. From the 1x4: one footer front 39" and two footer sides 11-1/4". From the 1x2: three face frame stiles 35-3/4" and one face frame top rail 39-1/2". From the 1/4" sheet: the 37-1/2" x 39" back panel, and hold off on the door panels until the doors are sized. Leave the 1x3 door frame parts uncut entirely for now — the plan's own note says to cut them to your actual openings, and on this build that is not boilerplate. Label the sides, top, bottom shelf and divider as they come off the saw; four of them are within four inches of each other and only the sides are a matched pair.

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Assemble the two 39-1/4" sides, the 39-1/2" top, the 36" bottom shelf and the 35-3/4" centre divider with 3/4" pocket holes, 1-1/4" pocket hole screws and glue, keeping every pocket hole where the face frame, the back or a shelf will later hide it. The 36" bottom shelf plus a 3/4" side at each end makes the case 37-1/2" across — which is exactly the width of the 1/4" back panel, so hold the back up to the case as your check before the glue sets. The 39-1/2" top then overhangs 1" at each end. Measure both diagonals and push on the long one until the two match: a wide case with a centre divider is stiff in every direction except the one that matters, and it will hold whatever shape it dries in.

steps.title: Attach the back and add the basket shelves
steps.body: Cut the 37-1/2" x 39" back and nail it on with glue and 1" brads every 6" into the sides, the top, the bottom shelf and the centre divider — square the case one more time first, because this panel is what locks the box permanently into whatever shape it is nailed to. Then fit the two 17-5/8" basket-bay shelves, which is the width of the open bay beside the divider. Fasten each one to the centre divider on one end and the side on the other, using pocket screws from underneath or brads and glue, and set them at the heights your baskets actually need rather than to a number — the plan's own note about the bottom shelf's position does not reconcile with the rest of its dimensions, so measure your baskets.

steps.title: Add the footer
steps.body: Attach the two 11-1/4" footer sides first, flush with the outside faces of the case sides, then the 39" footer front across them — glue plus 1-1/4" brads into the case bottom and into the ends of the side pieces. Fitting the sides first means the front piece hides their end grain at the corners. Keep the top edge of the footer tight against the underside of the case all the way round; a gap there reads as a shadow line and undoes the built-in look the footer exists to create.

steps.title: Build and attach the face frame
steps.body: Build the face frame as its own flat assembly on the bench, joined with pocket holes — this is far easier to keep square than framing in place on the box. Once it's square and dry, glue and nail the completed frame onto the front of the cabinet.

steps.title: Build and hang the doors
steps.body: Measure your two actual door openings off the finished face frame before you cut a single door part, because the cut list's door numbers do not close against this cabinet: the centre divider leaves a 17-5/8" cabinet bay, so two doors across it come out under 9" wide each, while the listed 16-1/4" rails and 21-1/4" stiles describe a door about 21" square and the 16" x 26" panels are longer than either reading allows. Take each opening off the frame, size the door to leave an even 1/8" gap all round, then cut the 1x3 frame parts and trim the 1/4" panel to suit. Assemble each frame around its panel flat on the bench, check it square, and hang it on the concealed hinges. Fit the knobs last, once the gaps are even and both doors close without binding.

steps.title: Sand and finish
steps.body: Take the doors off and pull the knobs before finishing — it is faster than cutting in around hardware and the edges get properly covered. Fill every visible nail hole and any gaps at the face frame joints, let the filler dry, then sand the whole piece through the grits, easing the front edges of the top and the shelves where hands and baskets pass. Get finish onto the underside of the top and the inside of the bays as well as the show faces, so the panels take up moisture evenly and the case stays flat. Rehang the doors and refit the knobs when everything is dry.

images.alt: Shanty open shelf console with center divider and two-door cabinet bay
